input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
Here's how to make your MAUI page theme-aware:

## XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />

        <Label Text="This page follows the system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#AAAAAA}" />

        <Button Text="Click Me"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

`AppThemeBinding` reacts to `Application.RequestedTheme` automatically — no event wiring needed. When the OS theme changes, every bound property updates in real time.

## C# alternative — `SetAppTheme`

The equivalent in code-behind or any C# context:

```csharp
// On any BindableObject (Label, Button, Page, etc.)
myLabel.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,   // Light value
    Colors.White    // Dark value
);

BackgroundColor = Application.Current!.RequestedTheme == AppTheme.Dark
    ? Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
    : Colors.White;

// For dynamic updates via SetAppTheme:
this.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")
);
```

`SetAppTheme` is the programmatic equivalent of `AppThemeBinding` — it registers reactive bindings that update automatically on theme change.

## Key points

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Follows `Application.Current.RequestedTheme` (OS setting) |
| **Values** | `Light`, `Dark`, and optional `Default` (fallback) |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` (colors, images, styles, etc.) |
| **No manual subscription** | Unlike `RequestedThemeChanged` event, these bindings are automatic |

**Tip:** For larger apps, define theme colors as resources in `App.xaml` and reference them, rather than hardcoding hex values on every element:

```xml
<!-- App.xaml -->
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">
        <!-- resolved via AppThemeBinding where used -->
    </Color>
</Application.Resources>

<!-- Or use merged styles -->
<Style TargetType="Label">
    <Setter Property="TextColor"
            Value="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#E0E0E0}" />
</Style>
```

This keeps your pages clean and your palette centralized.
